project.name: dashboard
project.description: Res.Publica Dashboard — a React + Vite + TypeScript single-page application that visualizes German and EU political data: a Bundestag voting hemicycle, election analyses, legislation tracking, an EU law explorer, a lobby register, and an interactive MapLibre world map. It consumes a public Node API and ships a custom design system with light/dark themes and DE/EN i18n.

nodes.name: ProjectionToggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Segmented toggle for switching the world map between projection modes, with disabled-state messaging.

nodes.name: ProjectionToggle
nodes.summary: Renders a localized segmented control of map projection options and emits the selected mode, supporting a disabled state with reason.

nodes.name: RadarChart.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG radar/spider chart rendering a single polygon over multiple labelled axes with grid rings.

nodes.name: RadarChart
nodes.summary: Projects axis values onto a polar coordinate system and draws the data polygon, grid levels, spokes, and axis labels as SVG.

nodes.name: SectionDivider.tsx
nodes.summary: Labelled horizontal section divider used to separate content groups within a page.

nodes.name: SectionDivider
nodes.summary: Renders a themed rule with an optional inline label for visually separating sections.

nodes.name: ShareCompact.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact share widget offering quick links to share the current page to social platforms plus copy-to-clipboard.

nodes.name: ShareCompact
nodes.summary: Builds encoded share URLs for several platforms and renders compact share buttons with a copy-link action and copied feedback.

nodes.name: ShareToolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Full share toolbar exposing native Web Share, social platform links, and copy-to-clipboard for the current page.

nodes.name: ShareToolbar
nodes.summary: Renders a row of localized share actions using the native share API when available, social deep links, and a clipboard copy with confirmation.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop collapsible sidebar navigation with grouped module entries, theme/language controls, and embedded share widget.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Renders the collapsible desktop navigation tree from grouped entries, handling module selection, collapse toggling, and theme/language switching.

nodes.name: Sparkline.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal inline SVG sparkline rendering a numeric series as a single trend line with optional min/max markers.

nodes.name: Sparkline
nodes.summary: Normalizes a numeric array to chart coordinates and draws a compact SVG trend line, optionally marking the extreme points.

nodes.name: StackedAreaChart.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG stacked area chart layering import and export trade volumes over time with hover tooltips.

nodes.name: StackedAreaChart
nodes.summary: Builds stacked import/export area polygons from a trade time-series and renders them as interactive SVG paths with a hover tooltip.

nodes.name: StatTile.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact statistic tile displaying a label, primary value, optional sub-text, and icon.

nodes.name: StatTile
nodes.summary: Renders a single themed metric tile with label, value, optional subtitle, and icon.

nodes.name: StatWidget.tsx
nodes.summary: Statistic widget variant displaying a label, value, sub-text, and icon, with an optional fluid (full-width) layout.

nodes.name: StatWidget
nodes.summary: Renders a themed key-metric widget supporting a fluid layout mode and optional icon and subtitle.

nodes.name: TradeBalance.tsx
nodes.summary: Trade balance summary block comparing total exports and imports and rendering a localized surplus/deficit verdict.

nodes.name: TradeBalance
nodes.summary: Compares export and import totals, computes the net balance, and renders an interpolated localized description of surplus or deficit.

nodes.name: TrendArrow.tsx
nodes.summary: Directional trend arrow indicating up/down/flat movement of a value, plus a helper to derive trend direction from two values.

nodes.name: TrendArrow
nodes.summary: Renders an up/down/neutral arrow with formatted percentage change, optionally inverting the color semantics.

nodes.name: trendDirectionFromValues
nodes.summary: Compares an older and newer numeric value and returns the corresponding trend direction.

nodes.name: ViewToggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic segmented view toggle for switching between mutually exclusive display options.

nodes.name: ViewToggle
nodes.summary: Renders a themed segmented control from an options list and emits the selected value on change.

nodes.name: VoteBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Bundestag vote breakdown bar showing yes/no/abstain/absent proportions for a faction with an expandable detail panel.

nodes.name: VoteBar
nodes.summary: Computes vote proportions from yes/no/abstain/absent counts and renders a segmented bar with a toggleable localized detail breakdown.

nodes.name: hsSections.ts
nodes.summary: Static lookup tables mapping HS commodity section codes to German and English human-readable labels.

nodes.name: tokens.ts
nodes.summary: Design-system token definitions: light and dark color palettes plus shared spacing, font, and breakpoint scales consumed across all components.
nodes.languageNotes: Central source of theming primitives imported by virtually every design-system component.

nodes.name: ThemeContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider for the design system that manages light/dark theme and DE/EN language state, persisting both to localStorage and exposing a theme palette, spacing tokens, and a translation function.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React context with useCallback/useMemo memoization and a useTheme hook guarded against missing provider.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: Provider component that holds theme and language state, syncs them to localStorage via effects, and supplies the merged theme/i18n context value to descendants.

nodes.name: Badge.tsx
nodes.summary: Themed inline badge/pill component rendering short status text in one of several semantic color variants.

nodes.name: Badge
nodes.summary: Renders a small labelled pill whose background and text colors are derived from the active theme and the chosen variant.

nodes.name: BalanceBarChart.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG trade-balance bar chart that plots yearly export/import net balance with hover interaction and localized currency formatting.

nodes.name: BalanceBarChart
nodes.summary: Computes a per-year net trade balance from export/import data and renders it as an interactive SVG bar chart with an active-bar tooltip.

nodes.name: DataCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic themed card container with optional header, footer, and clickable/active states used to wrap dashboard content blocks.

nodes.name: DataCard
nodes.summary: Renders a bordered card with optional header/footer slots and keyboard-accessible click handling when interactive.

nodes.name: EmptyState.tsx
nodes.summary: Themed placeholder shown when a data view has no results, optionally rendering a call-to-action element.

nodes.name: EmptyState
nodes.summary: Renders centered placeholder text with an optional action node for empty data views.

nodes.name: FilterToolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable filter/search toolbar that renders a text search field plus a set of declarative select filters for list pages.

nodes.name: FilterToolbar
nodes.summary: Builds a themed search-and-filter bar from a declarative filter definition array, wiring each control to its onChange handler.

nodes.name: HBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Horizontal bar component that visualizes a single value relative to a max, with label, formatted value, and optional icon.

nodes.name: HBar
nodes.summary: Renders a labelled horizontal progress-style bar whose fill width is the value clamped to a maximum.

nodes.name: HSSectionBreakdown.tsx
nodes.summary: Trade breakdown widget that ranks HS commodity sections by export or import value, with partner selection and responsive layout.

nodes.name: HSSectionBreakdown
nodes.summary: Aggregates and sorts HS-section trade rows by value, renders ranked bars with localized USD formatting, and integrates a partner picker on wider viewports.

nodes.name: InfoToggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Small expandable info/help toggle that shows or hides an explanatory text block on click.

nodes.name: InfoToggle
nodes.summary: Renders an info button that toggles the visibility of an inline explanatory panel using local open state.

nodes.name: LegalFooter.tsx
nodes.summary: Site-wide legal footer that renders data-source attributions for the current route plus imprint and licensing links.

nodes.name: LegalFooter
nodes.summary: Derives the active page from the router location, looks up its source catalog entries, and renders localized attribution and legal links.

nodes.name: LoadingSpinner.tsx
nodes.summary: Themed CSS loading spinner shown while async data is being fetched.

nodes.name: LoadingSpinner
nodes.summary: Renders a theme-colored animated spinner element for loading states.

nodes.name: MobileNav.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile slide-out navigation drawer with module entries, theme and language switches, and embedded share controls.

nodes.name: MobileNav
nodes.summary: Renders a toggleable mobile nav drawer that lists navigation entries, dispatches selection, and exposes theme/language and share actions.

nodes.name: MultiLineChart.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG dual-line chart plotting export and import trade series over time with hover tooltips and localized axis labels.

nodes.summary: Projects cleaned trade time-series into SVG polyline paths for exports and imports and renders an interactive hover tooltip.

nodes.name: PageHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Standard page header rendering a title and optional subtitle with consistent design-system typography.

nodes.name: PageHeader
nodes.summary: Renders a themed page title with an optional subtitle line.

nodes.summary: Pagination control rendering numbered page buttons with ellipsis truncation and previous/next navigation.

nodes.summary: Computes the list of page numbers and ellipsis placeholders to display for a given current page and total page count.

nodes.name: PartnerPicker.tsx
nodes.summary: Dropdown selector for choosing a trade partner country, displaying each partner with localized total trade value.

nodes.name: PartnerPicker
nodes.summary: Renders a themed partner select control listing partner names with formatted USD trade volumes and emits selection changes.

nodes.name: ProgressBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-segment horizontal progress bar that renders proportional colored segments from a segments definition.

nodes.name: ProgressBar
nodes.summary: Renders a stacked horizontal bar composed of proportionally-sized colored segments.

nodes.summary: Table presenting a district's party vote shares across historical election years, highlighting the winning party per row.

nodes.summary: Autocomplete search input for German districts (Kreise) that filters GeoJSON features by name and exposes a reusable hit-filtering helper.

nodes.name: MapMode.tsx
nodes.summary: Map-centric election view orchestrating the choropleth map, metric/type selectors, district search, and the advanced analysis tabs.

nodes.summary: Map view orchestrating the choropleth, metric/type selectors, district search, and analysis tabs.

nodes.summary: Main analysis tab component wiring scatter/ranking/change views, managing tab state, region search, and gain/loss leaderboards.

nodes.summary: SVG choropleth map coloring districts by vote-share change with hover tooltips and zoom-to-region.

nodes.summary: Side panel showing a selected region's election detail: per-party bars, time series across years, and metadata, fetched from the API.

nodes.summary: Configurable scatter plot correlating two district metrics with selectable axes and party/state color modes, plus an interactive color legend.

nodes.name: mapColors.ts
nodes.summary: Color-scale functions mapping election metrics (party share, turnout, vote change) to fill colors for choropleth maps.

nodes.name: mapGeometry.ts
nodes.summary: GeoJSON projection utilities converting district coordinates into SVG path data and building a lookup map of district geometries.

nodes.name: RankingTable
nodes.summary: Sortable ranking table of districts by a metric with state derivation from AGS codes.

nodes.name: RegionPanel
nodes.summary: Side panel rendering a selected region's election detail with bars, time series, and metadata.

nodes.name: rowToBarData
nodes.summary: Transforms an election row into per-party bar-chart data entries.

nodes.name: ScatterPlot
nodes.summary: Configurable scatter plot correlating two district metrics with selectable axes and color modes.

nodes.name: ScatterColorLegend
nodes.summary: Interactive color legend for the scatter plot's party/state color modes.

nodes.name: scatterAxisLabel
nodes.summary: Returns a localized axis label for a scatter plot metric key.

nodes.name: TimeSeriesChart
nodes.summary: Multi-party line chart plotting vote shares over election years with comparison overlays.

nodes.name: typeLabelT
nodes.summary: Returns a localized label for an election type via the translation function.

nodes.name: sectionTitle
nodes.summary: Renders a styled section-title element for the elections layout.

nodes.name: turnoutColor
nodes.summary: Maps a turnout value within a range to a sequential fill color.

nodes.name: changeColor
nodes.summary: Maps a vote-change delta to a diverging fill color around a neutral midpoint.

nodes.name: mapFillColor
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the appropriate color scale based on the active map metric.

nodes.name: projectCoords
nodes.summary: Projects geographic coordinates into SVG pixel space within a bounding box.

nodes.name: featureToPath
nodes.summary: Converts a GeoJSON feature's geometry into an SVG path data string.

nodes.name: buildKreiseMap
nodes.summary: Builds a lookup map of district SVG path data and bounds from a GeoJSON collection.

nodes.name: normalizeWahlen.ts
nodes.summary: Normalization helpers converting raw API election values into display percentages and resolving district display names.

nodes.name: partyColors.ts
nodes.summary: Central palette and labels for German parties and federal states, with light/dark theme variants and AGS-to-state-color helpers.

nodes.name: regionBarRows.ts
nodes.summary: Helper transforming a region's election row into bar-chart data using shared party colors and normalization utilities.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for election map rows, distinguishing API-shaped rows from normalized display rows.

nodes.name: resolveKreisDisplayName
nodes.summary: Resolves a district's display name from AGS code, name map, and API-provided name.

nodes.name: normalizeMapRow
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw API map row into a display-ready row with percentages and names.

nodes.name: regionRowToSingleResultBars
nodes.summary: Transforms a region's election row into single-result bar-chart data.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that wires up React Router with the dashboard layout shell, theme provider, and lazy-loaded routes for every module (elections, Bundestag, legislation, EU law, world map, lobby register, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React.lazy + Suspense for code-splitting heavy route pages while keeping lightweight stub pages eagerly imported.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default-exported root component defining the BrowserRouter route table that maps German URL slugs to the dashboard's page components under a shared DashboardLayout.

nodes.name: AbstimmungsDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the detailed breakdown of a single Bundestag vote (Abstimmung), showing each parliamentary group's yes/no/abstain/absent tallies sorted in political left-to-right order with party-derived colors.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a family of fuzzy fraction-name matching helpers (normalizeFraktion, matchesOrderToken, matchesSitzRow) to reconcile inconsistent German party labels coming from the API.

nodes.name: shortFraktionName
nodes.summary: Maps a raw API party/fraction label to a canonical short display name (CDU/CSU, SPD, AfD, Grüne, etc.) via case-insensitive substring and regex matching.

nodes.name: matchesOrderToken
nodes.summary: Tests whether an API party label matches a given political-order token using normalized substring and word-boundary checks.

nodes.name: matchesSitzRow
nodes.summary: Determines whether an API party label corresponds to a given seat-distribution row, reconciling differing CDU/CSU and party-name spellings.

nodes.name: labelColorFromSitzverteilung
nodes.summary: Resolves the brand color for a party label by locating its matching row in the official seat-distribution data.

nodes.name: labelColorForLegendDot
nodes.summary: Produces the legend-dot color for a party, adjusting near-white CDU fills for visibility against the active theme background.

nodes.name: sortFraktionenByPoliticalOrder
nodes.summary: Returns a copy of the fraction-vote list sorted by canonical political left-to-right index.

nodes.name: AbstimmungsDetail
nodes.summary: Main component rendering the per-fraction vote table for one Bundestag poll, including legend dots, tallies, and localized headers.

nodes.name: AbstimmungsListe.tsx
nodes.summary: Searchable, paginated list of Bundestag votes (Abstimmungen) that lets the user filter by title and select a poll to inspect in detail.

nodes.name: AbstimmungsListe
nodes.summary: Component that filters the supplied votes by a search query, shows a capped visible slice with a show-all toggle, and emits the selected poll id to the parent.

nodes.name: Hemicycle.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the Bundestag seating chart (hemicycle) as an SVG, coloring each seat by party and overlaying per-seat vote results with reveal animation; the project's central parliamentary visualization.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines raw seat geometry (RAW_SEATS) with live API seat-distribution and vote data through layered fraction-name canonicalization and useMemo-derived render arrays.

nodes.name: canonicalKey
nodes.summary: Collapses the many spellings of a German party/fraction name into a single canonical lookup key used to merge seat and vote data.

nodes.name: mergePartiesForLegend
nodes.summary: Aggregates seat-distribution rows by canonical party key into legend entries with combined seat counts and representative fill colors.

nodes.name: orderedLegend
nodes.summary: Orders the merged legend entries into political left-to-right sequence, appending any unranked parties by descending seat count.

nodes.name: matchApiRowForSeat
nodes.summary: Finds the API seat-distribution row that best matches a given seat label using exact then fuzzy substring matching.

nodes.name: partyFillForSeat
nodes.summary: Determines the fill color for an individual seat, preferring the matched API party color and falling back to the raw seat color.

nodes.name: matchFraktionVote
nodes.summary: Locates the fraction-level vote tally that matches a seat's party so individual seats can be colored by how their group voted.

nodes.name: voteColorForSeatInPartei
nodes.summary: Computes a seat's vote color by distributing a fraction's yes/no/abstain/absent counts across its seats by local index.

nodes.name: buildSeatDerived
nodes.summary: Transforms the static RAW_SEATS geometry into per-seat derived records with stable ids and local party-relative indices.

nodes.name: Hemicycle
nodes.summary: Main component that assembles seat geometry, party fills, vote colors, and an animated reveal into the interactive SVG hemicycle with legend and seat selection.

nodes.name: bundestag-seats.ts
nodes.summary: Static dataset exporting the raw geometric coordinates and party category for every Bundestag seat (RAW_SEATS) plus the total seat count, used to draw the hemicycle.
nodes.languageNotes: A large generated constant array; no logic, purely seat geometry data.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the entire design system: theme context, charts, and all reusable UI components plus their prop types for convenient single-source imports.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure re-export barrel with 53 named exports and no runtime logic.

nodes.name: useSearchParamsState.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that binds a single URL query-string parameter to component state, returning the current value and a setter that updates the search params.

nodes.name: useSearchParamsState
nodes.summary: Hook reading a named search param via react-router and exposing a memoized setter that deletes the param on empty values.

nodes.name: DashboardLayout.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level layout shell that renders the sidebar navigation (or mobile nav), derives the active module from the current path, and hosts the routed page content via React Router's Outlet.

nodes.name: DashboardLayout
nodes.summary: Default-exported layout component building the localized navigation entries, handling responsive sidebar collapse, and rendering the active route through an Outlet.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap entry that mounts the App component into the DOM root with React StrictMode and loads global CSS.

nodes.name: Admin.tsx
nodes.summary: Placeholder admin page rendering a localized page header and phase-placeholder message; a stub awaiting future admin functionality.

nodes.name: Admin
nodes.summary: Default-exported placeholder admin page component showing a header and a localized placeholder paragraph.

nodes.name: Bundestag.tsx
nodes.summary: Bundestag page that orchestrates the vote browser: fetches votes, seat distribution, and deputy data via the API, and composes the AbstimmungsListe, AbstimmungsDetail, and Hemicycle components with deep-linkable poll selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges API party labels to the static seat dataset via canonicalKey/apiFraktionToRawKategorie matching before passing data down to the visualization components.

nodes.name: canonicalKey
nodes.summary: Normalizes a party/fraction name into a canonical key for matching API data against the static seat dataset.

nodes.name: apiFraktionToRawKategorie
nodes.summary: Maps an API fraction label to the corresponding raw seat-category key used by the static RAW_SEATS geometry.

nodes.name: Bundestag
nodes.summary: Default-exported page component managing poll selection state, fetching all Bundestag data via useApi, and wiring the list, detail, and hemicycle views together with route-param deep linking.

nodes.name: Coalition.tsx
nodes.summary: Placeholder page for the coalition agreement (Koalitionsvertrag) showing a localized header and phase-placeholder text.

nodes.name: Coalition
nodes.summary: Default-exported placeholder coalition page rendering a header and localized placeholder paragraph.

nodes.name: DemocracyIndex.tsx
nodes.summary: Placeholder page for the democracy index module displaying a localized header and phase-placeholder message.

nodes.name: DemocracyIndex
nodes.summary: Default-exported placeholder democracy-index page rendering a header and localized placeholder paragraph.

nodes.name: EuParliament.tsx
nodes.summary: Placeholder page for the EU Parliament module showing a localized header and phase-placeholder text.

nodes.name: EuParliament
nodes.summary: Default-exported placeholder EU Parliament page rendering a header and localized placeholder paragraph.

nodes.name: LobbyRegister.tsx
nodes.summary: Large, data-rich lobby register page that lists registered lobbying organizations with search and pagination and visualizes aggregate statistics through D3, MapLibre maps, and Recharts charts.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 2000+ line component combining maplibre-gl maps, d3, and recharts; the heaviest page in the app, hence lazy-loaded in App.tsx.

nodes.name: LobbyRegister
nodes.summary: Default-exported page component fetching lobby list and statistics via useApi, syncing filters to URL search params, and rendering tables, charts, and an interactive map of lobbying activity.

nodes.name: NotFound.tsx
nodes.summary: 404 fallback page rendered for unmatched routes, showing a localized not-found message and a link back to the dashboard.

nodes.name: NotFound
nodes.summary: Default-exported 404 component rendering a localized message and a navigation link home.

nodes.name: Overview.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard landing page that fetches headline statistics and latest items across modules (laws, EU law, Bundestag votes) and presents them as stat widgets and data cards with navigation shortcuts.

nodes.name: Overview
nodes.summary: Default-exported landing component aggregating cross-module statistics and recent items via useApi and rendering the dashboard overview with stat widgets and clickable cards.

nodes.name: EuCourtCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Expandable card component rendering a single EU Court of Justice case, lazy-loading full case detail (leitsatz, keywords, linked legal acts) from the API on expand.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses local helper functions (trunc, courtColors, courtLabel, keywordsToList) plus React hooks for an expand-on-click pattern with mobile-aware behavior.

nodes.name: EuCourtCard
nodes.summary: React component for an EU court case card with expand/collapse, theming, bilingual summaries, and on-demand fetch of case detail via useApi.

nodes.name: EuLawCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Expandable card component for a single EU legal act (regulation/directive/decision), showing type badges, EuroVoc tags, bilingual summaries, and navigation to linked German laws.
nodes.languageNotes: eurovocToStrings normalizes a polymorphic API field (string, object with label, or JSON-encoded array) into a flat string list.

nodes.name: eurovocToStrings
nodes.summary: Normalizes the polymorphic EuroVoc tag field (string, label-object array, or JSON string) into a clean array of label strings.

nodes.name: EuLawCard
nodes.summary: React component rendering an EU legal-act card with expand/collapse, type-coloured badges, EuroVoc tags, and router navigation to linked German laws.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for EU-law domain entities: legal-act list items, court cases, detail responses, and linked-law references.

nodes.name: GesetzDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Detail panel for a single German law, rendering metadata, summary, context, a coloured diff synopsis, linked lobby actors, and related court rulings.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a local DiffSynopse sub-component that line-colours unified-diff text and a formatMoney helper for lobby spend figures.

nodes.name: DiffSynopse
nodes.summary: Renders a unified-diff string as a colour-coded synopsis, highlighting added, removed, and header lines per the active theme.

nodes.name: GesetzDetail
nodes.summary: Main detail-view component composing law metadata, collapsible context/synopsis sections, linked lobby entries, and sorted related court rulings.

nodes.name: UrteilCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Card component for a single German court ruling (Urteil) with collapsible tenor/impact sections, legal-area badges, and links to referenced laws.

nodes.name: UrteilCard
nodes.summary: React component for a court-ruling card that lazy-loads ruling detail, renders collapsible tenor/impact text, and exposes clickable badges for referenced laws.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for the legislation domain: Gesetz (law) and Urteil (court ruling) record shapes consumed by gesetze components.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for legislation and court data: legal-area classification from law abbreviations, court badge variants, ISO date formatting, and URL/endpoint builders.

nodes.name: rechtGebietFromKuerzel
nodes.summary: Maps a normalized law abbreviation to its German legal area (civil, criminal, constitutional, tax/labour) using curated lookup sets.

nodes.name: formatDisplayDate
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO date string into a locale-aware display date (DE/EN), with a manual fallback when Intl formatting is unavailable.

nodes.name: buildUrteilListEndpoint
nodes.summary: Constructs the court-ruling list API endpoint from optional court and legal-area filters, URL-encoding query parameters.

nodes.name: useApi.ts
nodes.summary: Core data-fetching hook that builds API URLs from a configurable base, fetches JSON with abort/retry handling, and exposes data/loading/error/refetch state.
nodes.languageNotes: Generic over the response type; uses AbortController and a setTimeout-based single retry inside the effect.

nodes.name: useApi
nodes.summary: Generic React hook performing an aborted JSON fetch for an endpoint, with retry-once-on-failure and a refetch callback, returning data/loading/error.

nodes.name: useDebouncedValue.ts
nodes.summary: Small React hook that returns a debounced copy of a value, updating only after the given delay elapses without changes.

nodes.name: useMediaQuery.ts
nodes.summary: React hook wrapping matchMedia to track a media query's match state, plus a useIsMobile convenience hook bound to the design-system mobile breakpoint.

nodes.name: useMediaQuery
nodes.summary: Subscribes to a CSS media query via matchMedia and returns its current match state, updating on change events.

nodes.name: Elections.tsx
nodes.summary: Elections page rendering an interactive district (Kreis) map with autocomplete search, party-result overlays, and theme-aware SVG geometry.

nodes.name: Elections
nodes.summary: Default-exported Elections page component coordinating district map geometry, autocomplete selection, and normalized election result rows from the API.

nodes.name: EuLaw.tsx
nodes.summary: EU-law page that lists EU legal acts and Court of Justice rulings with filtering, search, pagination, and aggregate counts by type and court.
nodes.languageNotes: Local helpers buildEuListUrl/buildEuUrteilListUrl assemble filtered API endpoints; countByTyp/countByGericht produce facet aggregates.

nodes.name: EuLaw
nodes.summary: Default-exported EU-law page orchestrating filtered/paginated fetches of legal acts and court rulings, rendering EuLawCard and EuCourtCard lists with facet counts.

nodes.name: buildEuListUrl
nodes.summary: Builds the EU legal-act list API endpoint from search, type, and pagination filters with URL-encoded parameters.

nodes.name: Legislation.tsx
nodes.summary: Legislation page listing German laws and court rulings with search, filtering, and a master-detail layout that opens GesetzDetail and UrteilCard views.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest file in the batch; includes a LegislationWelcome intro sub-component and several list-normalization/title-derivation helpers.

nodes.name: Legislation
nodes.summary: Default-exported Legislation page managing law/ruling lists, search and filter state, selection, and the detail panel composing GesetzDetail and UrteilCard.

nodes.name: LegislationWelcome
nodes.summary: Intro/welcome panel shown when no law is selected, explaining the legislation explorer and its data sources.

nodes.name: WorldMap.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive MapLibre world-map page rendering country-level indicators with projection switching, floating widget panels, and Safari/globe capability detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes feature-detection helpers (isLegacyIosSafari, isGlobeProjectionSupported) and localStorage persistence for visible floating widgets.

nodes.name: WorldMap
nodes.summary: Default-exported world-map page wiring a MapLibre instance, projection mode, country data overlays, and persisted floating widget configuration.

nodes.name: isGlobeProjectionSupported
nodes.summary: Feature-detection helper determining whether the runtime supports MapLibre globe projection, guarding against legacy iOS Safari.

nodes.name: parseVisibleFloatingWidgets
nodes.summary: Parses the persisted set of visible floating map widgets from storage, validating entries against the known widget keys.

nodes.name: worldFilters.ts
nodes.summary: Small predicate helpers for world-map data filtering: detecting real countries and checking for World Bank region presence across rows.

nodes.name: sourcesCatalog.ts
nodes.summary: Static catalog mapping each dashboard page to its curated list of data sources, plus a helper that derives the footer source-line i18n key from a route pathname.

nodes.name: footerSourcesLineKey
nodes.summary: Resolves the i18n key for a page's footer source line by parsing the route pathname into its leading path segment.

nodes.name: CompareBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Design-system component rendering a labeled horizontal comparison bar for country vs. region vs. world values with a configurable value formatter.

nodes.name: CompareBar
nodes.summary: React component that normalizes country/region/world values against their max and renders themed comparison bars with formatted labels.

nodes.name: LineChart.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive SVG line chart with optional area fill, gridlines, annotations, and a hover tooltip that tracks the cursor across data points.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds the polyline/area paths manually from data coordinates and uses useCallback-memoized pointer handlers for hover hit-testing.

nodes.name: LineChart
nodes.summary: React component that scales a data series into an SVG viewport, draws the line/area/annotations, and computes a hovered point from pointer position.

nodes.name: MonoLabel.tsx
nodes.summary: Small design-system primitive rendering uppercase monospaced label text styled from the active theme tokens.

nodes.name: PercentileBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Design-system component visualizing a percentile value as a filled bar with optional inversion and a localized footer caption.

nodes.name: PercentileBar
nodes.summary: React component that renders a themed percentile bar, supporting inverted scales and an interpolated localized footer label.

nodes.name: i18n.ts
nodes.summary: Aggregating i18n barrel that merges the per-feature DE/EN message modules into unified dictionaries and provides the placeholder interpolation helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Spreads disjoint feature message objects and derives the I18nKey union via keyof typeof de for type-safe translation keys.

nodes.name: interpolate
nodes.summary: Replaces {name} placeholders in a translated string with values from a vars record, coercing each value to a string.

nodes.name: common.ts
nodes.summary: Shared DE/EN translation dictionaries for cross-cutting UI labels (navigation, common actions, shared components) used across the dashboard.

nodes.name: elections.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the elections feature labels and copy.

nodes.name: eu.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the EU law feature (legislation, court rulings, and related UI copy).

nodes.name: gesetze.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the German legislation (Gesetze) feature labels and descriptions.

nodes.name: lobby.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the lobby register feature labels and explanatory copy.

nodes.name: overview.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the dashboard overview/landing page labels.

nodes.name: sources.ts
nodes.summary: DE/EN translation dictionaries for the data Sources page labels, captions, and footer source lines.

nodes.name: world.ts
nodes.summary: Largest DE/EN translation dictionary covering the world map feature, including country indicators, metric labels, and map UI copy.
nodes.languageNotes: By far the largest i18n module (~589 lines), reflecting the breadth of country-level metrics surfaced on the MapLibre world map.

nodes.name: Sources.tsx
nodes.summary: Sources page that lists each dashboard feature's curated data sources, combining the static sources catalog with live API-reported worldmap sources and their freshness and value counts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat and Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware freshness and value-count rendering, with useMemo to assemble worldmap source rows.

nodes.name: sectionHeading
nodes.summary: Renders a themed section heading with an anchor link and stable heading id for a sources block.

nodes.name: formatRelativePast
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO timestamp as a locale-aware relative-past string (minutes/hours/days/months) using Intl.RelativeTimeFormat.

nodes.name: renderStaticSourcesBlock
nodes.summary: Renders a full static sources section from a catalog block, mapping each source entry into a titled, badged, and linked list item.

nodes.name: Sources
nodes.summary: Top-level Sources page component that fetches live worldmap sources, merges them with the static catalog, and renders all per-feature source sections with freshness metadata.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow triggered on push to main and on every pull request, running TypeScript typecheck, production build, and lint as quality gates.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-job workflow. The build job uses npm run build as a hard gate (tsc -b && vite build) while npm run lint runs with continue-on-error so it never blocks CI.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: The 'Typecheck, build, lint' job on ubuntu-latest: checks out the repo, sets up Node 20, installs dependencies with npm ci, runs the production build, then runs lint informationally.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation (in German) covering the tech stack, all SPA routes/pages, the design system, API conventions, and the local development and deploy workflow for the Res.Publica Dashboard.

nodes.name: deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Bash deploy script that pulls the latest main branch, installs dependencies with npm ci, and runs the production Vite build into the Nginx-served dist directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses 'set -e' to abort on first error and appends timestamped progress lines to deploy.log.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry document providing the #root mount point, Google Fonts preconnect/stylesheet links, favicon, and the module script tag that bootstraps the React app from src/main.tsx.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest defining the dev/build/lint/preview scripts and pinning runtime dependencies (React, react-router-dom, d3, recharts, maplibre-gl) and the TypeScript/Vite/ESLint dev toolchain.
nodes.languageNotes: ES module package ('type': 'module'); build script chains 'tsc -b' project references with 'vite build'.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript solution-style config that holds no files of its own and delegates to the tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json project references.
nodes.languageNotes: Solution-style config using TypeScript project references rather than direct file inclusion.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the application source under src/, enabling strict mode, bundler module resolution, react-jsx, and ES2020 target with DOM libs.
nodes.languageNotes: Bundler mode with allowImportingTsExtensions and noEmit, delegating actual emit to Vite.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration scoped to the Node-side build tooling (vite.config.ts), targeting ES2022 with strict linting and bundler module resolution.

nodes.name: .cursorrules
nodes.summary: Cursor AI editor rules file defining project conventions and coding guidelines for AI-assisted development in the Res.Publica dashboard repository.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file for the Understand-Anything analysis tool, excluding build artifacts, dependencies, and generated files from knowledge-graph scanning.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Minimal configuration for the Understand-Anything plugin, setting the output language for generated analysis content.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat config wiring up TypeScript, React Hooks, and React Refresh rules for linting the dashboard source tree.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the modern ESLint flat config format (flat array of config objects) rather than the legacy .eslintrc.

nodes.name: BUG8-gesetz-titel-TODO.md
nodes.summary: Developer TODO note documenting a UX bug where the legislation list view shows raw file identifiers instead of human-readable display titles, with the open decision and implementation steps.

nodes.name: world.geojson
nodes.summary: GeoJSON dataset of world country boundaries served as a static asset, used by the MapLibre world map to render political-data choropleths.
nodes.languageNotes: GeoJSON FeatureCollection consumed client-side by MapLibre rather than imported as a module.

nodes.name: useLocalStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that persists a state value to localStorage, hydrating from storage on mount and writing back on every update.

nodes.name: useLocalStorage
nodes.summary: Generic hook returning a stateful value and setter that transparently syncs with localStorage via JSON serialization, falling back to a default value when no entry exists.

nodes.name: useOffsetPagination.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook providing offset-based pagination state (page, limit, computed offset) with page-setting and reset helpers for paginated API list views.

nodes.name: useOffsetPagination
nodes.summary: Returns an OffsetPagination object that derives the query offset from the current page and limit, exposing setPage and reset callbacks for list-driven data fetching.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global base stylesheet imported by the application entry point, establishing root-level CSS resets and baseline styling for the SPA.

nodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript ambient declaration file referencing Vite client types so that import.meta.env and asset imports are correctly typed across the project.
nodes.languageNotes: Triple-slash directive (/// <reference types="vite/client" />) pulling in Vite's ambient module and env typings.

nodes.name: ConsoleComponents.jsx
nodes.summary: Standalone UI-kit prototype defining reusable themed visualization primitives (sparklines, line/radar charts, KPI tiles, percentile and comparison bars) for the Country Console, exposed as globals on window.
nodes.languageNotes: Non-module prototype: components are assigned to window.X = function rather than exported, relying on global React/useTheme/FONTS/SPACE bindings declared via /* global */ comments.

nodes.name: Sparkline
nodes.summary: SVG sparkline component that maps a numeric series to a polyline with an end dot and optional peak/trough markers, scaling data to the given width and height.

nodes.name: LineChart
nodes.summary: SVG single-series line chart with optional area fill, grid lines, year-axis labels, and vertical event annotations for time-series indicator data.

nodes.name: CompareBar
nodes.summary: Grouped horizontal bar component comparing a country value against regional and world values, normalizing each bar against the group maximum.

nodes.name: CountryConsole.jsx
nodes.summary: Large standalone Country Console prototype assembling indicator formatting helpers and a global ranking view from country statistics, building on the ConsoleComponents UI primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: Prototype JSX relying on globally-bound React/useTheme and helpers (de-DE Intl number formatting) rather than ES module imports.

nodes.name: trendFromValues
nodes.summary: Computes a relative trend ratio from the tail of an indicator's value series, returning null when there is insufficient data.

nodes.name: fmtUsd
nodes.summary: Formats a USD numeric value into a compact human-readable string (billions/millions) using locale-aware number formatting.

nodes.name: fmtPopulation
nodes.summary: Formats a population count into a compact scaled string (millions/thousands) using locale-aware de-DE number formatting.

nodes.name: GlobalView
nodes.summary: Top-level global ranking view component that derives top-5 and bottom-5 country rankings from a ranking list and renders them with themed bars and formatted values.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration registering the React plugin and project-level build/dev-server options for the dashboard SPA.

nodes.name: toDisplayPercent
nodes.summary: Normalizes a vote-share/turnout value to a display percentage: scales 0–1 decimals by 100 and passes through values already expressed as percent, returning 0 for null/NaN.

nodes.name: useDebouncedValue
nodes.summary: Generic React hook that returns a debounced copy of a value, updating only after the given delay in milliseconds has elapsed without further changes.

nodes.name: useTheme
nodes.summary: React hook that reads the ThemeContext, returning theme, language, colors and translation helpers; throws if used outside a ThemeProvider.

nodes.name: MonoLabel
nodes.summary: Presentational component rendering its children as a small, uppercase, letter-spaced monospaced label using theme-derived muted color.

layers.name: Application Shell & Routing
layers.description: Bootstraps the SPA and wires React Router: the root App component with lazy-loaded module routes, the main.tsx mount point, the DashboardLayout shell, and global base CSS.

layers.name: Feature Pages & Components
layers.description: Route-level pages (Bundestag, Elections, Legislation, EU Law, World Map, Lobby Register, Overview, Sources) plus their feature-specific components, charts, maps, and colocated helpers/types for the German/EU political modules.

layers.name: Design System
layers.description: The reusable component library and theming foundation: design tokens, the ThemeContext (light/dark + DE/EN i18n), the per-feature i18n dictionaries, and shared UI primitives, charts, navigation, and layout components.

layers.name: Hooks & Data Access
layers.description: Custom React hooks encapsulating shared stateful logic and the public Node API client, including useApi for data fetching plus localStorage, debounce, media-query, pagination, and URL-param state hooks.

layers.name: Static Data & Assets
layers.description: Static datasets and pure data utilities consumed by the visualizations: Bundestag seat geometry, the sources catalog, world GeoJSON, world-map filter predicates, and standalone UI-kit prototypes.

layers.name: Configuration & Build
layers.description: Project configuration and build tooling: package.json, the TypeScript project configs, the Vite and ESLint configs, Vite ambient type declarations, and AI/tooling config files.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Deployment and continuous-integration assets: the GitHub Actions workflow that typechecks, builds, and lints, plus the Nginx-targeted production deploy script.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including the German README covering stack, routes, and deploy workflow, and developer TODO/bug notes.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README, the project's German-language guide to the Res.Publica Dashboard. It explains that this is a React 18 + Vite 5 + TypeScript single-page app that visualizes German and EU political data (Bundestag votes, elections, legislation, EU law, a lobby register, and a world map), and it walks through the tech stack, the SPA routes, the design system, and the API conventions. Read this first to get the mental map the rest of the tour fills in with real code.

tour.title: Boot and Mount
tour.description: Two small files turn a static HTML page into a running React app. index.html provides the #root mount point and the module script tag, and main.tsx is the bootstrap that mounts the App component into that root inside React StrictMode and pulls in the global base stylesheet. Together they are the literal first lines of code the browser runs.
tour.languageLesson: Vite uses native ES modules: index.html loads /src/main.tsx as a <script type="module">, and the bundler resolves the import graph from there. React 18's createRoot(...).render(<StrictMode><App/></StrictMode>) double-invokes effects in development to surface side-effect bugs early.

tour.title: Routing Root
tour.description: App.tsx is the heart of the application shell and the top of the dependency graph. It wires up React Router, wraps everything in the theme provider, and declares every route, eagerly loading the light pages while lazy-loading the heavy modules (Bundestag, World Map, Lobby Register). Building on the boot step, this is where you see the full menu of features the dashboard offers.
tour.languageLesson: React.lazy(() => import('./pages/Bundestag')) defers a module's JavaScript until its route is visited, code-splitting the heavy visualization pages into separate chunks. A <Suspense> boundary supplies the fallback UI shown while that chunk downloads.

tour.title: The Layout Shell
tour.description: DashboardLayout is the frame every page renders inside. It chooses between the desktop Sidebar and the mobile navigation drawer, derives the active module from the current URL path, and hosts the routed page content through React Router's Outlet. This is the persistent chrome (navigation, theme and language switches) that surrounds the feature pages from App.tsx.

tour.title: Design Tokens and Theming
tour.description: These three files are the most depended-upon in the entire codebase: tokens.ts (73 importers) defines the light and dark color palettes plus spacing and font scales, while ThemeContext.tsx (44 importers) is the React context that manages light/dark theme and DE/EN language state, persists both to localStorage, and exposes the palette and a translation function. The design-system index.ts barrel re-exports the whole library so feature code can import everything from one place. Understand these and you understand the visual and behavioral vocabulary shared by every component.
tour.languageLesson: React Context lets a provider broadcast theme and language state to any descendant via a useTheme() hook, avoiding prop-drilling. Barrel files (index.ts re-exporting with 'export * from') create a single clean import surface for the whole module.

tour.title: Internationalization
tour.description: The dashboard is fully bilingual (German/English), and the i18n system shows how that scales. The i18n.ts barrel merges eight per-feature message modules into unified DE/EN dictionaries and provides the placeholder-interpolation helper that ThemeContext's translation function calls. Splitting messages by feature (common, elections, eu, gesetze, lobby, overview, sources, world) keeps each module focused and was the subject of a recent refactor noted in the git history.

tour.title: Data Access via the API Hook
tour.description: Almost every page gets its data through useApi.ts (16 importers), the core data-fetching hook. It builds request URLs from a configurable API base, fetches JSON with abort and retry handling, and returns data/loading/error/refetch state for components to render against. The supporting hooks here (debounce for search inputs, media-query for responsive layout, offset pagination for long lists) round out the shared stateful logic that feature pages lean on.
tour.languageLesson: A custom hook is a function whose name starts with 'use' and that calls other hooks. useApi encapsulates a useEffect that creates an AbortController, fires the fetch, and cancels the request on unmount or dependency change, preventing state updates on stale responses.

tour.title: Feature Spotlight: Bundestag Hemicycle
tour.description: The Bundestag module is the project's signature visualization. The Bundestag page fetches votes, seat distribution, and deputy data via useApi and composes three pieces: AbstimmungsListe (a searchable, paginated vote browser), AbstimmungsDetail (per-faction yes/no/abstain/absent tallies), and Hemicycle, the SVG seating chart that colors each seat by party and animates the per-seat vote result. The seat geometry comes from the static bundestag-seats dataset. This is a textbook example of the page-orchestrates-components pattern used throughout the app.

tour.title: Feature Spotlight: Elections Map
tour.description: The elections module is the largest feature by file count and shows how a complex page is decomposed. The Elections page renders an interactive choropleth of Germany's districts (Kreise) with autocomplete search and party overlays. Its shared building blocks recur across the module: partyColors.ts (18 importers) is the canonical party and federal-state palette, types.ts (16 importers) distinguishes raw API rows from normalized display rows, and normalizeWahlen.ts (14 importers) converts raw values into display percentages. Studying these reveals how the team factors out cross-cutting data shapes and color logic.

tour.title: Feature Spotlight: World Map and Lobby Register
tour.description: Two data-rich features round out the picture. WorldMap renders country-level indicators on a MapLibre map with projection switching, floating widget panels, and Safari/globe capability detection, backed by the static world.geojson boundary dataset and worldFilters helpers. LobbyRegister lists registered lobbying organizations with search and pagination and visualizes aggregate statistics with D3, MapLibre, and Recharts. Both are lazy-loaded (per App.tsx) precisely because they pull in heavy mapping and charting libraries.

tour.title: Feature Spotlight: Legislation and EU Law
tour.description: These two modules cover the legal side of the dashboard and share a master-detail pattern. The Legislation page lists German laws and court rulings and opens GesetzDetail (a law's metadata, summary, diff synopsis, linked lobby actors, and related rulings). The EuLaw page lists EU legal acts and Court of Justice cases through expandable cards that lazy-load full detail on demand. The Overview page ties the modules together, fetching headline stats and latest items across laws, EU law, and Bundestag votes for the landing screen.

tour.title: Static Data and Source Attribution
tour.description: Beyond fetched data, the dashboard relies on curated static catalogs. sourcesCatalog.ts maps each page to its list of data sources and derives the footer attribution key from the route, feeding both the LegalFooter on every page and the dedicated Sources page (which augments the static catalog with live API-reported world-map sources and freshness counts). This is where the project's commitment to data provenance lives in code.

tour.title: Build Configuration
tour.description: With the application understood, look at how it is built. package.json pins the runtime stack (React, react-router-dom, d3, recharts, maplibre-gl) and defines the dev/build/lint/preview scripts; vite.config.ts registers the React plugin and build options; and the TypeScript project references (root tsconfig.json delegating to the strict app and node configs) govern type-checking. These files define the toolchain every other step depends on.
tour.languageLesson: tsconfig.json uses solution-style project references: it holds no files itself and points at tsconfig.app.json (the src/ application, ES2020 + DOM) and tsconfig.node.json (the Vite tooling, ES2022). This lets the editor and CLI type-check browser code and build scripts under different settings in one repo.

tour.title: CI and Deployment
tour.description: The tour ends where every change does: the pipeline that ships it. The GitHub Actions workflow runs on every push to main and every pull request, setting up Node 20 and running typecheck, production build, and lint as quality gates. The companion deploy.sh script pulls the latest main, installs with npm ci, and runs the Vite production build into the Nginx-served dist directory described in the README. Together they close the loop from the code you just toured to the live app at app.respublica.media.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use 'on' triggers (push, pull_request) and 'jobs' made of sequential 'steps'. Running build and typecheck as required gates before merge catches type errors and broken builds in CI rather than in production.
